Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab is a disease affecting Apple plants that can reduce plant health and crop yield if left unmanaged.
Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Typical symptoms include leaf discoloration, spots, lesions, wilting, or abnormal growth associated with Apple scab.
Apple___Apple_scab.causes: This condition is commonly caused by fungal, bacterial, viral pathogens or pests depending on Apple scab.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Remove infected plant parts, apply suitable fungicide, bactericide, insecticide, or other recommended control measures, and follow local agricultural guidance.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Use disease-free planting material, ensure proper spacing, avoid excess moisture, rotate crops, and inspect plants regularly.
